name: Working Person's Crockpot Country Pork Dinner
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare all ingredients the night before and assemble in the morning before leaving for work.
2. In a large plastic bag, combine flour, salt, and pepper. Coat the pork loin country-style ribs with the flour mixture; reserve any leftover flour.
3. In a large skillet over medium-high heat, add hot salad oil. Cook the meat in batches until well browned on all sides, removing pieces as they brown.
4. Reduce heat to medium and stir the reserved flour into the drippings in the skillet. Gradually stir in apple cider. Simmer until thickened to a gravy consistency; remove from heat and set aside.
5. In the 6-quart crockpot, add potatoes, carrots, onions, cabbage, and caraway seeds.
6. Top with meat and pour on the gravy.
7. Cover and cook on high for 6 hours or low for 8-10 hours.
